questions.promptHtml: A global financial services company requires centralized identity management for 50,000 employees across 20 subsidiaries. Each subsidiary demands autonomy over user lifecycle management while maintaining corporate security policies. The solution must support conditional access and PIM across all entities.

options.label: Single Azure AD tenant with administrative units
Azure AD B2B with multiple guest tenants
Separate Azure AD tenant per subsidiary with B2B collaboration

questions.correctLabel: Single Azure AD tenant with administrative units
questions.extraHtml: Administrative units enable delegated administration within a single tenant while maintaining centralized governance, conditional access, and PIM policies. Multiple tenants fragment identity management and complicate cross-subsidiary collaboration. B2B is for external partners, not internal subsidiaries requiring full lifecycle management.

questions.promptHtml: An application processes sensitive healthcare data and must encrypt secrets at rest with customer-managed keys rotated every 90 days. Key operations must be auditable with hardware-backed protection. Access requires approval from two security officers.

options.label: Azure Key Vault Premium with managed HSM and access policies
Azure Key Vault Standard with Azure RBAC and rotation policies
Azure Dedicated HSM with custom key management

questions.correctLabel: Azure Key Vault Premium with managed HSM and access policies
questions.extraHtml: Premium tier provides HSM-backed keys (FIPS 140-2 Level 3). Access policies support multi-person authorization for key operations. Managed HSM offers full control over HSM resources. Standard tier lacks HSM backing. Azure Dedicated HSM is for legacy applications requiring exclusive HSM access, adding unnecessary complexity.

questions.promptHtml: A multinational corporation needs to enforce that all Azure Cosmos DB accounts use private endpoints and prohibit public network access across 200 subscriptions spanning 15 business units. Compliance must be audited monthly with automatic remediation.

options.label: Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists effect and compliance reporting
Azure Blueprints with locked resource groups
Management group hierarchy with Azure RBAC deny assignments

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists effect and compliance reporting
questions.extraHtml: Azure Policy at management group scope enforces configuration across subscriptions. DeployIfNotExists automatically remediates non-compliant resources. Built-in compliance dashboard tracks violations. Blueprints are for initial deployment, not ongoing enforcement. RBAC deny assignments prevent actions but don't remediate existing configurations.

questions.promptHtml: A SaaS application serves 10,000 customer organizations, each requiring isolated identity domains with custom branding and MFA policies. Customers must federate their corporate identity providers (SAML/OIDC). Authentication must handle 100,000 logins per day with sub-second response times.

options.label: Azure AD B2C with custom policies and identity providers
Azure AD B2B with external identities
Multiple Azure AD tenants with Azure Front Door

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD B2C with custom policies and identity providers
questions.extraHtml: B2C is purpose-built for customer identity with multi-tenancy, custom branding per tenant, IDP federation (SAML/OIDC), and custom policies for complex authentication flows. Scales to millions of users. B2B is for business partnerships, not customer-facing multi-tenant SaaS. Multiple AD tenants don't provide isolation per customer organization.

questions.promptHtml: An enterprise must collect, analyze, and retain Azure Activity Logs, resource logs, and metrics from 500 resources across multiple regions for 7 years to meet regulatory requirements. Queries must support complex analytics with sub-second performance on recent data.

options.label: Azure Monitor Logs workspace with data export to Azure Storage Archive
Azure Event Hubs with Stream Analytics to Cosmos DB
Azure Storage Archive with direct query capability

questions.correctLabel: Azure Monitor Logs workspace with data export to Azure Storage Archive
questions.extraHtml: Log Analytics workspace provides real-time querying with KQL for recent data. Export to Archive Storage reduces costs for long-term retention (7 years). Event Hubs doesn't provide log aggregation or long-term storage. Storage Archive alone lacks query capabilities without rehydration.

questions.promptHtml: A microservices application on AKS requires distributed tracing across 50 services, with automatic dependency mapping, performance anomaly detection, and correlation of user transactions across services. Telemetry must integrate with Prometheus metrics.

options.label: Application Insights with distributed tracing and OpenTelemetry
Azure Monitor Logs with custom KQL queries
Grafana with Prometheus exporters

questions.correctLabel: Application Insights with distributed tracing and OpenTelemetry
questions.extraHtml: Application Insights provides native distributed tracing, automatic dependency mapping, smart detection for anomalies, and transaction correlation. OpenTelemetry integration supports Prometheus metrics ingestion. Azure Monitor Logs lacks automatic application topology mapping. Grafana/Prometheus requires manual instrumentation and lacks transaction correlation.

questions.promptHtml: A healthcare organization requires Azure resources to be organized for chargeback to 5 business divisions, 20 departments, and 100 cost centers. Resource deployments must enforce tagging, with automatic cost analysis dashboards per hierarchy level. Non-compliant resources must be blocked at creation.

options.label: Management groups with Azure Policy tag inheritance and Cost Management
Subscriptions per department with Azure RBAC
Resource groups per cost center with manual tagging

questions.correctLabel: Management groups with Azure Policy tag inheritance and Cost Management
questions.extraHtml: Management group hierarchy mirrors organizational structure. Azure Policy enforces required tags with Deny effect (blocks non-compliant deployments) and inherit tags from parent scopes. Cost Management automatically aggregates costs by tags. Subscriptions per department don't scale to 100 cost centers. Manual tagging lacks enforcement.

questions.promptHtml: A financial application must ensure privileged access to production subscriptions requires just-in-time activation with multi-factor authentication, business justification, and automatic expiration after 4 hours. All activations must trigger alerts to SOC.

options.label: Azure AD Privileged Identity Management with approval workflow
Azure RBAC with time-bound role assignments
Azure AD Conditional Access with MFA

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD Privileged Identity Management with approval workflow
questions.extraHtml: PIM provides JIT activation, multi-factor authentication at activation, approval workflows, business justification requirements, time-bound access (configurable duration), and activation alerts. Azure RBAC doesn't support JIT or approval workflows natively. Conditional Access enforces MFA but lacks JIT and time-bound access.

questions.promptHtml: An IoT solution ingests telemetry from 1 million devices at 10,000 events/second. Monitoring must detect device anomalies in real-time, track device connectivity patterns, and generate alerts when event ingestion drops below 8,000 events/second for 5 minutes.

options.label: Azure Monitor metrics with alert rules and action groups
Application Insights with custom events and smart detection
Log Analytics workspace with scheduled KQL queries

questions.correctLabel: Azure Monitor metrics with alert rules and action groups
questions.extraHtml: Azure Monitor metrics natively collect IoT Hub/Event Hub metrics (events per second, device connections). Alert rules support threshold-based alerts with time aggregation (5-minute windows). Action groups route alerts to SOC. Application Insights is for application telemetry, not infrastructure metrics. Log queries add latency vs. real-time metrics.

questions.promptHtml: A government agency must ensure all Azure SQL databases in 80 subscriptions enable Transparent Data Encryption with customer-managed keys, audit logging to immutable storage, and Advanced Threat Protection. Non-compliant databases must be automatically configured, and compliance must be visible in a central dashboard.

options.label: Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists and compliance dashboard
Azure Security Center with security policies
Azure Blueprints with ARM templates

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists and compliance dashboard
questions.extraHtml: Azure Policy DeployIfNotExists effect automatically remediates non-compliant resources by deploying required configurations. Built-in policies exist for SQL TDE, auditing, and ATP. Compliance dashboard aggregates status across subscriptions. Security Center recommends configurations but doesn't enforce or auto-remediate. Blueprints are for initial provisioning, not ongoing compliance.

questions.promptHtml: A web application uses Managed Identity to access Azure Key Vault, Cosmos DB, and Storage. The application runs in AKS and must minimize privilege by granting access only to specific Key Vault secrets, Cosmos DB containers, and Storage containers. Identity should not require credential management.

options.label: User-assigned Managed Identity with Azure RBAC at resource scope
System-assigned Managed Identity with access policies
Service Principal with certificate authentication

questions.correctLabel: User-assigned Managed Identity with Azure RBAC at resource scope
questions.extraHtml: User-assigned MI can be shared across pods and provides lifecycle management independent of workload. Azure RBAC grants granular permissions (e.g., specific secrets, containers). No credential management required. System-assigned MI is tied to single resource lifecycle. Service Principal requires certificate rotation and management.

questions.promptHtml: A multi-tenant application must authorize users to access resources in their own tenant based on custom claims (e.g., department, clearance level) evaluated dynamically from an HR system API during each request. Authorization rules must be centrally managed and auditable.

options.label: Azure AD with custom security attributes and Conditional Access policies
API Management with policy expressions and external authorization service
Application code with JWT claim validation

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD with custom security attributes and Conditional Access policies
questions.extraHtml: Custom security attributes allow storing custom claims (department, clearance) in Azure AD. Conditional Access policies evaluate claims dynamically per-request with external API integration. Centrally managed in Azure AD with audit logs. API Management doesn't provide identity-aware authorization at scale. Application code lacks centralized management and auditability.

questions.promptHtml: An organization has 100 subscriptions across 10 Azure AD tenants due to acquisitions. A project requires cross-tenant resource access with centralized identity management, unified billing, and consistent governance policies. Migration to a single tenant is not feasible in the short term.

options.label: Azure Lighthouse with delegated resource management
Azure AD B2B with guest access to each tenant
Azure Resource Mover to consolidate subscriptions

questions.correctLabel: Azure Lighthouse with delegated resource management
questions.extraHtml: Lighthouse enables cross-tenant management with Azure RBAC delegation. Managing tenant sees all resources in a unified view, enforces policies, and provides centralized billing. B2B guest access doesn't consolidate management or governance. Resource Mover relocates resources but doesn't solve multi-tenant governance challenges.

questions.promptHtml: A machine learning application requires access to thousands of API keys, database passwords, and certificates across development, staging, and production environments. Secrets must be versioned, access must be audited, and applications must retrieve secrets without code changes when rotating.

options.label: Azure Key Vault with secret versioning and references in App Configuration
Azure App Service application settings with slot settings
Azure DevOps variable groups with secured variables

questions.correctLabel: Azure Key Vault with secret versioning and references in App Configuration
questions.extraHtml: Key Vault provides secret versioning, audit logs, automatic rotation notifications, and retrieval via URI (no code changes). App Configuration centrally manages references to Key Vault secrets per environment. App Service settings lack versioning and audit. DevOps variable groups are for CI/CD, not runtime secret access.

questions.promptHtml: A compliance requirement mandates recording every privileged operation (e.g., role assignments, policy changes, key access) across 200 Azure subscriptions with tamper-proof storage for 10 years. Logs must be searchable for forensic investigations.

options.label: Azure Activity Logs exported to Log Analytics with immutable storage archive
Azure Monitor with data export to Event Hubs
Azure Storage with append-only blob containers

questions.correctLabel: Azure Activity Logs exported to Log Analytics with immutable storage archive
questions.extraHtml: Activity Logs capture control plane operations (RBAC, policy, etc.). Export to Log Analytics enables KQL queries for investigations. Archive to immutable Storage (WORM policy) ensures tamper-proof retention for 10 years. Event Hubs is for streaming, not long-term storage. Append-only blobs lack query capabilities.

questions.promptHtml: An application requires conditional access policies that deny access from non-corporate devices unless the user is in a specific security group and the application risk level is low. MFA must be required for all external network access regardless of device compliance.

options.label: Azure AD Conditional Access with multiple conditions and controls
Azure AD Identity Protection with risk-based policies
Azure Firewall with application rules

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD Conditional Access with multiple conditions and controls
questions.extraHtml: Conditional Access supports multiple conditions (device compliance, group membership, risk level, network location) combined with AND/OR logic. Controls enforce MFA or block access. Identity Protection calculates risk but doesn't enforce multi-condition policies. Azure Firewall operates at network layer, not identity layer.

questions.promptHtml: A global organization must route Azure Monitor Logs from 500 resources in 10 regions to separate Log Analytics workspaces per region for data residency compliance, with a central workspace for global queries. Regional logs must never leave their geography.

options.label: Diagnostic settings per resource with workspace destinations and cross-workspace queries
Azure Event Hubs with regional namespaces and Stream Analytics
Azure Data Factory with regional pipelines copying logs

questions.correctLabel: Diagnostic settings per resource with workspace destinations and cross-workspace queries
questions.extraHtml: Diagnostic settings send resource logs to region-specific workspaces (ensures data residency). Cross-workspace queries in Log Analytics aggregate data for global analysis without moving data. Event Hubs adds complexity without log retention. Data Factory is for data movement, violating residency requirements.

questions.promptHtml: A containerized application on AKS must authenticate to Azure SQL, Key Vault, and Cosmos DB using Managed Identity without storing credentials. Different pods must have different permissions based on workload identity. Solution must support Kubernetes RBAC integration.

options.label: Azure AD Workload Identity with federated credentials
System-assigned Managed Identity on AKS cluster
Service Principal per pod with Kubernetes secrets

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD Workload Identity with federated credentials
questions.extraHtml: Workload Identity federates Kubernetes service accounts with Azure AD identities, enabling per-pod identity with Managed Identity benefits. Integrates with Kubernetes RBAC. System-assigned MI applies cluster-wide, not per-pod. Service Principals require credential management in secrets.

questions.promptHtml: An enterprise must enforce that all resource groups in 50 subscriptions inherit tags (CostCenter, Environment, Owner) from parent management groups. New resource groups without these tags must be denied creation. Existing non-compliant groups must be tagged automatically.

options.label: Azure Policy with Deny and Modify effects at management group scope
Azure Resource Manager templates with required parameters
Azure Automation runbook triggered by Event Grid

questions.correctLabel: Azure Policy with Deny and Modify effects at management group scope
questions.extraHtml: Azure Policy Deny effect prevents creation of non-compliant resource groups. Modify effect auto-remediates existing resources by adding missing tags. Inherit tag policies copy tags from management group. ARM templates don't enforce tags at scale. Runbooks add operational overhead and delay.

questions.promptHtml: A workload requires monitoring 200 VMs for CPU, memory, disk, and network metrics with sub-minute granularity. Alerts must trigger when average CPU exceeds 80% across all VMs for 3 minutes or when any single VM disk space drops below 10%. Custom performance counters must be collected.

options.label: Azure Monitor VM Insights with metrics and alert rules
Log Analytics with performance counters and scheduled queries
Azure Monitor Logs with diagnostics extension

questions.correctLabel: Azure Monitor VM Insights with metrics and alert rules
questions.extraHtml: VM Insights automatically collects detailed performance metrics with 1-minute granularity, including custom counters, with minimal configuration. Alert rules support multi-resource conditions (average across VMs) and single-resource thresholds. Log-based monitoring adds query latency vs. near-real-time metrics.

questions.promptHtml: A regulated workload must ensure administrators cannot assign themselves privileged roles without approval. All role activations require justification and time-bound access (maximum 8 hours). Security team must review all activations weekly.

options.label: Azure AD Privileged Identity Management with approval workflow and access reviews
Azure Policy with role assignment restrictions
Azure RBAC with custom roles and deny assignments

questions.correctLabel: Azure AD Privileged Identity Management with approval workflow and access reviews
questions.extraHtml: PIM enforces approval workflows for role activation, requires justification, and limits access duration (configurable up to 8 hours). Access reviews provide periodic certification of privileged access. Azure Policy can't control role activation workflows. RBAC doesn't support approval or JIT.

questions.promptHtml: A financial application stores transaction records in Azure SQL Database with millions of INSERTs per day. Data older than 90 days is rarely queried but must be retained for 7 years for compliance. Recent data requires sub-second query performance. Solution must minimize costs.

options.label: SQL Database with temporal tables and automatic data archiving to Azure Storage
SQL Database with partitioning and read replicas
Cosmos DB with TTL and change feed to Archive Storage

questions.correctLabel: SQL Database with temporal tables and automatic data archiving to Azure Storage
questions.extraHtml: Temporal tables provide automatic historical data tracking. Data retention policies automatically move old data to cheaper Azure Storage (reducing SQL storage costs) while maintaining queryability. Partitioning improves performance but doesn't reduce costs. Cosmos DB TTL deletes data, not archives it.

questions.promptHtml: A global e-commerce platform requires a database with single-digit millisecond reads/writes from 20 geographic regions, with automatic conflict resolution for concurrent updates. Data must be strongly consistent in the primary region and eventually consistent globally. RPO must be zero.

options.label: Azure Cosmos DB with multi-region writes and custom conflict resolution
Azure SQL Database with active geo-replication
Azure Database for PostgreSQL with read replicas

questions.correctLabel: Azure Cosmos DB with multi-region writes and custom conflict resolution
questions.extraHtml: Cosmos DB supports multi-region writes with configurable consistency levels (strong in primary, eventual globally). Custom conflict resolution policies (last-writer-wins, custom procedures) handle concurrent updates. Zero RPO with synchronous replication in write regions. SQL geo-replication requires manual failover and doesn't support multi-write. PostgreSQL replicas are read-only.

questions.promptHtml: A data warehouse stores 500 TB of historical sales data queried monthly for executive reports. Queries analyze 3-5 years of data with complex aggregations. Current solution uses Azure SQL Database at DTU premium tier with monthly costs exceeding $15,000. Queries can tolerate 30-second start latency.

options.label: Azure Synapse Analytics serverless SQL pool with data in Azure Data Lake Storage
Azure SQL Database with read-scale-out replicas
Azure Cosmos DB with analytical store

questions.correctLabel: Azure Synapse Analytics serverless SQL pool with data in Azure Data Lake Storage
questions.extraHtml: Serverless SQL pool charges only for data scanned during queries (pay-per-query), not continuous compute. Ideal for infrequent querying of massive datasets in ADLS Gen2. Supports complex SQL analytics. Read replicas reduce primary load but don't reduce costs. Cosmos DB analytical store suited for operational analytics, not data warehouse scale.

questions.promptHtml: A SaaS application serves 10,000 tenants with isolated databases per tenant (database-per-tenant model). Each tenant database is 5-50 GB. The solution must maximize resource utilization, simplify management, and provide per-tenant performance isolation with automatic scaling.

options.label: Azure SQL Database elastic pools with per-database min/max eDTU settings
Azure SQL Managed Instance with collocation
Separate SQL Database servers per tenant

questions.correctLabel: Azure SQL Database elastic pools with per-database min/max eDTU settings
questions.extraHtml: Elastic pools share resources across multiple databases (cost efficiency) while allowing per-database limits (performance isolation). Automatically scales resources based on aggregate demand. Simplifies management (single pool vs. thousands of servers). Managed Instance doesn't offer per-database resource controls. Separate servers maximize cost and management complexity.

questions.promptHtml: A machine learning workload stores 10 PB of unstructured training data (images, videos) accessed randomly during model training. Data is write-once, read-many with training jobs requiring maximum throughput. Data must be encrypted with customer-managed keys and support lifecycle management to archive data after 180 days.

options.label: Azure Blob Storage Premium block blobs with lifecycle management and CMK
Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with hierarchical namespace
Azure Files Premium with SMB protocol

questions.correctLabel: Azure Blob Storage Premium block blobs with lifecycle management and CMK
questions.extraHtml: Premium block blobs provide highest throughput for random access workloads. Lifecycle management policies automatically move data to Archive tier after 180 days. Supports CMK encryption. ADLS Gen2 optimized for analytics (sequential reads), not random access ML training. Azure Files suited for shared file systems, not blob-scale ML datasets.

questions.promptHtml: A healthcare application stores millions of DICOM medical images (each 50-500 MB) that must be available for immediate retrieval during patient visits. Images older than 5 years are accessed less than once per year but must remain retrievable within 15 hours. Solution must minimize storage costs.

options.label: Azure Blob Storage Hot tier with lifecycle policy to Archive tier after 5 years
Azure Blob Storage Cool tier with manual migration
Azure Files with tiering to Azure NetApp Files

questions.correctLabel: Azure Blob Storage Hot tier with lifecycle policy to Archive tier after 5 years
questions.extraHtml: Hot tier provides immediate access for recent images. Lifecycle policies automatically transition blobs to Archive tier after 5 years (lowest cost). Archive retrieval supports rehydration within 15 hours. Cool tier more expensive than Archive for rarely accessed data. Azure Files doesn't support automatic tiering to low-cost archive.

questions.promptHtml: A financial services application stores audit logs as JSON files in Azure Storage, totaling 50 TB annually. Logs must be immutable for 7 years (WORM compliance), queryable without moving data, and support granular access control per compliance team reviewing specific log types.

options.label: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with immutable blob storage and Azure RBAC
Azure Blob Storage with legal hold and shared access signatures
Azure Cosmos DB with analytical store and TTL

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with immutable blob storage and Azure RBAC
questions.extraHtml: ADLS Gen2 immutable storage provides time-based retention (WORM) for compliance. Native querying via Synapse/Databricks without data movement. Hierarchical namespace enables folder-level RBAC for granular access control. Legal hold doesn't enforce time-based retention automatically. Cosmos DB unsuited for massive log file storage.

questions.promptHtml: A media streaming platform stores 100 TB of video files in Azure Storage, accessed by 1 million users globally. Content must be delivered with low latency worldwide, with 95% of requests served within 50ms. Storage costs must be minimized while maintaining high availability.

options.label: Azure Blob Storage with Azure CDN Premium and geo-redundant storage
Azure Storage with read-access geo-redundant storage and geofiltering
Azure Files Premium with global file sync

questions.correctLabel: Azure Blob Storage with Azure CDN Premium and geo-redundant storage
questions.extraHtml: Azure CDN caches content at edge locations globally (low-latency delivery). Premium CDN provides advanced caching rules and performance optimization. GRS provides storage redundancy. RA-GRS without CDN can't meet 50ms latency globally. Azure Files Premium lacks CDN integration for web delivery.

questions.promptHtml: A data analytics workload processes 5 TB of IoT telemetry daily, stored as Parquet files in Azure Storage. Queries filter by device ID, timestamp, and sensor type. Data scientists run ad-hoc queries requiring sub-second results filtering on metadata without scanning entire files.

options.label: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with Azure Synapse Analytics and partition pruning
Azure Blob Storage with Azure Data Explorer
Azure Table Storage with partition and row keys

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 with Azure Synapse Analytics and partition pruning
questions.extraHtml: ADLS Gen2 hierarchical namespace enables folder partitioning by device/timestamp. Synapse serverless SQL pools support partition pruning (only scans relevant partitions), dramatically reducing query time and cost. Data Explorer suited for time-series, not file-based analytics. Table Storage doesn't support Parquet or complex queries.

questions.promptHtml: A enterprise data warehouse ingests data from 50 on-premises SQL Server databases, 20 SaaS applications (Salesforce, SAP), and 100 Azure services. Data must be transformed and loaded nightly into Azure Synapse Analytics. Solution requires no-code data integration with monitoring and error handling.

options.label: Azure Data Factory with mapping data flows and monitoring pipelines
Azure Databricks with Spark jobs and notebooks
Azure Logic Apps with connectors and workflows

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Factory with mapping data flows and monitoring pipelines
questions.extraHtml: Data Factory provides 90+ native connectors for on-premises, SaaS, and Azure sources. Mapping data flows offer no-code visual ETL. Built-in monitoring, alerting, and retry logic. Databricks requires code (Spark) and lacks pre-built connectors. Logic Apps suited for workflows, not large-scale ETL.

questions.promptHtml: A retail company has data in Azure SQL Database, Cosmos DB, and Data Lake Storage that must be combined for Power BI dashboards with sub-second query performance. Data refreshes every 15 minutes. Business users require self-service querying without understanding underlying storage technologies.

options.label: Azure Synapse Analytics with serverless SQL pools and logical data warehouse
Azure Data Factory with incremental copies to Synapse dedicated pool
Power BI DirectQuery to each data source

questions.correctLabel: Azure Synapse Analytics with serverless SQL pools and logical data warehouse
questions.extraHtml: Serverless SQL pools create unified views across SQL, Cosmos, and ADLS without data movement. Supports T-SQL queries over heterogeneous sources. Integrates natively with Power BI. Dedicated pools require data movement (latency, cost). DirectQuery to multiple sources lacks performance optimization and unified schema.

questions.promptHtml: A financial application stores transactional data in Azure SQL Database (100 GB, 10,000 transactions/sec) and requires read-only analytics queries without impacting OLTP performance. Analytics queries scan large date ranges and execute every 5 minutes. Solution must minimize costs and replication lag.

options.label: SQL Database Hyperscale with read-scale-out replicas
SQL Database with active geo-replication and read replica
Cosmos DB with analytical store

questions.correctLabel: SQL Database Hyperscale with read-scale-out replicas
questions.extraHtml: Hyperscale read replicas provide near-zero replication lag, isolate analytics workload from primary OLTP, and scale independently. Supports up to 4 read replicas included in pricing. Geo-replication designed for disaster recovery, not analytics offloading, with higher replication lag. Cosmos DB requires data migration from SQL.

questions.promptHtml: A healthcare organization stores patient records in Azure SQL Database and must replicate data to Azure Synapse Analytics nightly for compliance reporting. Data contains PII that must be masked during replication. Failed pipelines must retry up to 3 times with alerts to operations team.

options.label: Azure Data Factory with masking transformations, retry policy, and alert actions
Azure SQL Data Sync with firewall rules
Azure Synapse pipeline with stored procedures

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Factory with masking transformations, retry policy, and alert actions
questions.extraHtml: Data Factory mapping data flows support column-level masking transformations. Pipeline retry policies handle transient failures. Alert actions integrate with email, Logic Apps, or webhook. SQL Data Sync for bidirectional sync between SQL databases, not analytics. Synapse pipelines lack built-in masking and sophisticated error handling.

questions.promptHtml: A manufacturing IoT solution captures time-series sensor data (temperature, pressure, vibration) from 100,000 devices at 1 event/second/device. Data must be stored for 1 year with queries aggregating by device, sensor type, and time windows. Solution must optimize for time-series queries and minimize costs.

options.label: Azure Data Explorer with time-series optimized storage and materialized views
Azure Cosmos DB with time-series containers
Azure SQL Database with time-series tables

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Explorer with time-series optimized storage and materialized views
questions.extraHtml: Data Explorer purpose-built for time-series analytics with columnar storage, automatic data compression, and time-series functions (aggregations, interpolation). Materialized views pre-compute aggregations for fast queries. Scales to billions of events. Cosmos DB expensive for massive time-series. SQL time-series lacks native time-series optimizations.

questions.promptHtml: A media company stores 50 TB of JSON event logs in Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2. Data scientists require SQL querying without moving data or provisioning databases. Queries execute 10-20 times per month analyzing specific date ranges (1-7 days of data per query).

options.label: Azure Synapse Analytics serverless SQL pool with OPENROWSET
Azure Databricks with Delta Lake
Azure Data Factory with data flows

questions.correctLabel: Azure Synapse Analytics serverless SQL pool with OPENROWSET
questions.extraHtml: Serverless SQL pools enable T-SQL queries directly over files in ADLS Gen2 using OPENROWSET (no data movement). Pay-per-query pricing ideal for infrequent usage. Supports JSON parsing. Databricks requires Spark knowledge and continuous compute. Data Factory for ETL, not ad-hoc querying.

questions.promptHtml: A global application stores customer profile data (name, address, preferences) totaling 10 TB. Data must be replicated across 5 Azure regions with automatic failover, strong consistency for reads/writes in the primary region, and eventual consistency in secondary regions. RPO must be zero in the primary region.

options.label: Azure Cosmos DB with multi-region writes and session consistency in primary region
Azure SQL Database with auto-failover groups and synchronous replication
Azure Storage with geo-redundant storage

questions.correctLabel: Azure Cosmos DB with multi-region writes and session consistency in primary region
questions.extraHtml: Cosmos DB supports multi-region writes with tunable consistency (strong in primary via bounded staleness, eventual in secondary). Automatic failover with RPO zero via synchronous replication in write region. SQL auto-failover limited to 2 regions with async secondary. GRS for blobs, not structured profile data with consistency requirements.

questions.promptHtml: A database workload requires 1 TB storage that doubles annually. Current Azure SQL Database General Purpose tier approaches max capacity. Workloads are transaction-heavy (50,000 IOPS) with unpredictable scaling. Solution must support growth to 100 TB without downtime and scale storage independently from compute.

options.label: Azure SQL Database Hyperscale tier
Azure SQL Managed Instance
Azure Cosmos DB with autoscale

questions.correctLabel: Azure SQL Database Hyperscale tier
questions.extraHtml: Hyperscale tier scales storage independently to 100 TB+ without downtime. Supports high IOPS (up to 100,000+) and instant snapshots. Storage auto-grows on demand. Managed Instance limited to 8-16 TB depending on hardware. Cosmos DB requires application refactoring for NoSQL model.

questions.promptHtml: A web application database experiences predictable load of 1,000 DTU during business hours (8 AM - 6 PM) and 100 DTU overnight. Database must automatically scale down overnight to reduce costs and scale up before business hours to maintain performance.

options.label: Azure SQL Database serverless tier with auto-pause and auto-resume
Azure SQL Database with Azure Automation scaling scripts
Azure SQL Elastic Pool with DTU-based scaling

questions.correctLabel: Azure SQL Database serverless tier with auto-pause and auto-resume
questions.extraHtml: Serverless tier automatically scales compute based on workload demand (1-100 DTU equivalent in vCores) and pauses during inactivity (no compute charges). Auto-resumes on first request. Ideal for intermittent workloads. Automation scripts add operational overhead and delay. Elastic pools for multi-database scenarios, not single database with predictable patterns.

questions.promptHtml: A video processing application encodes uploaded videos (1 TB per day) and stores results in Azure Blob Storage. Processing occurs in batches nightly. Current solution uses locally redundant storage (LRS). Business requires protection against regional failures with 4-hour RPO and automatic failover.

options.label: Geo-redundant storage (GRS) with read-access geo-redundant storage (RA-GRS)
Zone-redundant storage (ZRS) with lifecycle management
Locally redundant storage with Azure Backup

questions.correctLabel: Geo-redundant storage (GRS) with read-access geo-redundant storage (RA-GRS)
questions.extraHtml: GRS replicates data to secondary region (300+ miles away), providing regional disaster recovery. RA-GRS enables read access to secondary region during primary outage. RPO typically 15 minutes (well within 4 hours). ZRS protects against zone failures in single region. Azure Backup for long-term retention, not geo-redundancy.

questions.promptHtml: A financial application requires hybrid connectivity between on-premises data center and Azure SQL Database with <10ms latency, private connectivity (no public internet), and throughput up to 10 Gbps. Solution must support multiple subscriptions and virtual networks.

options.label: ExpressRoute with private peering and VNet integration
Site-to-site VPN with VPN Gateway
Azure SQL Database private endpoint with VPN

questions.correctLabel: ExpressRoute with private peering and VNet integration
questions.extraHtml: ExpressRoute provides dedicated private connection (bypasses internet) with predictable latency <10ms and bandwidth up to 100 Gbps. Supports VNet peering across subscriptions. Site-to-site VPN limited to 1.25 Gbps per tunnel with variable latency over internet. Private endpoint provides private IP but doesn't solve hybrid connectivity.

questions.promptHtml: A mission-critical application requires Azure SQL Database to withstand datacenter failures within a region with RPO of zero and RTO under 1 minute. The database must continue operating without data loss during zone outages.

options.label: Azure SQL Database Business Critical tier with zone-redundant configuration
Azure SQL Database with active geo-replication
Azure SQL Managed Instance with zone redundancy

questions.correctLabel: Azure SQL Database Business Critical tier with zone-redundant configuration
questions.extraHtml: Business Critical tier with zone redundancy replicates database synchronously across 3 availability zones (RPO zero). Automatic failover occurs within 30-60 seconds (RTO <1 min). Geo-replication provides regional redundancy but asynchronous (RPO >0). Managed Instance zone redundancy available but for instance-level, not matching single database requirement.

questions.promptHtml: A containerized microservices application on AKS must remain available during node failures, planned maintenance, and zone outages. Services must autoscale from 10 to 100 pods based on CPU and custom metrics (queue depth). Solution must support multiple node pools across availability zones.

options.label: AKS with system node pool zone-spanning and user node pools per zone with HPA and KEDA
Azure Container Instances with Azure Front Door
App Service for Containers with scaling rules

questions.correctLabel: AKS with system node pool zone-spanning and user node pools per zone with HPA and KEDA
questions.extraHtml: AKS system node pool across zones ensures control plane HA. User node pools distributed across zones provide workload resilience. HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) scales on CPU/memory. KEDA scales on custom metrics (queue depth, etc.). ACI doesn't support Kubernetes orchestration. App Service for Containers limited container orchestration capabilities.

questions.promptHtml: A data warehouse on Azure Synapse Analytics (dedicated SQL pool) stores 50 TB of historical sales data with nightly ETL loads. Business requires point-in-time restore capability for 30 days and long-term retention of year-end snapshots for 7 years. Restore must complete within 2 hours.

options.label: Automatic restore points with user-defined restore points and long-term backup to Azure Storage
Azure Backup with Recovery Services Vault
Geo-redundant storage replication

questions.correctLabel: Automatic restore points with user-defined restore points and long-term backup to Azure Storage
questions.extraHtml: Synapse dedicated pools provide automatic restore points every 8 hours (retained 7 days) and support user-defined restore points before critical operations (retained 42 days). Export database before year-end to Azure Storage for 7-year retention. Restore from point completes in 1-2 hours. Azure Backup doesn't support Synapse. GRS replicates data, not point-in-time recovery.

questions.promptHtml: A web application on App Service connects to Azure SQL Database. Business requires 4-hour RPO, 1-hour RTO, and protection against regional failures. Failover must be automatic without manual intervention. Solution must minimize costs during normal operation.

options.label: App Service with Traffic Manager and SQL Database with auto-failover groups
App Service slots with Azure Front Door and SQL geo-replication
Azure Site Recovery for App Service and SQL mirroring

questions.correctLabel: App Service with Traffic Manager and SQL Database with auto-failover groups
questions.extraHtml: Traffic Manager distributes traffic across regional App Service instances. SQL auto-failover groups provide automatic failover of database to secondary region with read-write listener endpoint (application connection string unchanged). Meets RPO/RTO requirements. Slots for staging, not DR. Site Recovery for VMs, not App Service PaaS.

questions.promptHtml: A file server workload stores 20 TB of user home directories on Azure Files Premium. Data must be protected with daily backups retained for 90 days and weekly backups for 5 years. Restores must support granular file-level recovery without restoring entire share.

options.label: Azure Backup for Azure Files with retention policies and item-level restore
Azure Files snapshots with lifecycle management
Azure Site Recovery with file-level recovery

questions.correctLabel: Azure Backup for Azure Files with retention policies and item-level restore
questions.extraHtml: Azure Backup provides policy-based backups (daily, weekly) with flexible retention (90 days, 5 years). Supports item-level restore (individual files/folders) without full share restore. Share snapshots manual and lack long-term retention management. Site Recovery for infrastructure DR, not file backup.

questions.promptHtml: A stateful application on Azure VMs requires crash-consistent backups every 6 hours with 30-day retention. During regional outage, VMs must be restored to secondary region within 2 hours. Backup solution must minimize impact on VM performance.

options.label: Azure Backup with snapshot-based incremental backups and cross-region restore
Azure Site Recovery with replication to secondary region
Managed disk snapshots with Azure Automation

questions.correctLabel: Azure Backup with snapshot-based incremental backups and cross-region restore
questions.extraHtml: Azure Backup uses instant restore snapshots (minimal performance impact) stored locally and vault-standard tier replicated to secondary region. Supports crash-consistent backups every 4 hours (configurable). Cross-region restore enables RTO 2 hours. ASR for continuous replication (lower RTO but higher cost). Manual snapshots lack orchestration and cross-region restore.

questions.promptHtml: A multi-tier application (web, app, database) on Azure VMs must failover to secondary region during disasters with RPO 15 minutes and RTO 4 hours. Failover must be orchestrated across all tiers with automated testing. Solution must support non-disruptive DR testing.

options.label: Azure Site Recovery with recovery plans and test failover
Azure Backup with cross-region restore
Geo-redundant load balancer with manual failover

questions.correctLabel: Azure Site Recovery with recovery plans and test failover
questions.extraHtml: ASR continuously replicates VMs to secondary region (RPO 15 minutes achievable). Recovery plans orchestrate multi-tier failover with sequencing and scripts. Test failover creates isolated environment for DR testing without affecting production. Azure Backup for data restore, not VM failover orchestration. Manual failover doesn't meet RTO requirements.

questions.promptHtml: A NoSQL database on Cosmos DB stores mission-critical application data with 99.999% availability SLA requirement. Data must be readable during regional outages with automatic failover and strong consistency within a region. Business operates in 3 Azure regions.

options.label: Cosmos DB with multi-region writes, availability zones, and bounded staleness consistency
Cosmos DB with single-region write and read regions
Cosmos DB with session consistency and manual failover

questions.correctLabel: Cosmos DB with multi-region writes, availability zones, and bounded staleness consistency
questions.extraHtml: Multi-region writes with availability zones enables 99.999% availability SLA. Bounded staleness consistency provides strong consistency within region (defined staleness window) while enabling multi-region writes. Automatic regional failover. Single-region write provides 99.99% SLA. Session consistency doesn't guarantee strong consistency.

questions.promptHtml: A real-time analytics application on Azure Synapse Analytics must remain available during datacenter failures within a region. Data warehouse stores 100 TB with nightly ETL jobs that cannot be interrupted. Solution must provide automatic failover within 5 minutes.

options.label: Synapse workspace with zone redundancy cannot meet requirements without additional architecture
Synapse dedicated SQL pool with geo-restore and Traffic Manager
Cosmos DB with analytical store

questions.correctLabel: Synapse workspace with zone redundancy cannot meet requirements without additional architecture
questions.extraHtml: Synapse dedicated pools don't currently support automatic zone redundancy or fast failover (geo-restore RTO hours, not minutes). Meeting <5 min RTO requires active-active architecture with data replication and manual failover orchestration. Geo-restore is backup-based (slow). This highlights Synapse limitation for HA within single region.

questions.promptHtml: A batch processing workload runs nightly ETL jobs processing 10 TB of data over 6 hours on 20 VMs. Jobs run once per day and do not require immediate response. VMs idle 18 hours daily. Solution must minimize compute costs while meeting nightly processing window.

options.label: Azure Batch with low-priority VMs and scale sets
Virtual Machine Scale Sets with deallocate during idle time
Azure Kubernetes Service with node auto-pause

questions.correctLabel: Azure Batch with low-priority VMs and scale sets
questions.extraHtml: Azure Batch optimized for large-scale batch processing with automatic scaling. Low-priority VMs provide up to 80% cost savings vs. standard VMs. Batch manages provisioning and deallocation. VMSS with manual deallocation requires scripting and doesn't optimize batch orchestration. AKS with node auto-pause not supported natively.

questions.promptHtml: A CPU-intensive scientific simulation requires 500 cores with RDMA interconnect for MPI workloads, completing in 48 hours. Workload runs quarterly. Solution must provide maximum CPU performance with lowest inter-node latency.

options.label: HB-series VMs with InfiniBand in Azure Batch
FSv2-series VMs with accelerated networking
Azure Container Instances with high CPU allocation

questions.correctLabel: HB-series VMs with InfiniBand in Azure Batch
questions.extraHtml: HB-series VMs purpose-built for HPC with high core count, InfiniBand interconnect (sub-microsecond latency), and RDMA support for MPI. Azure Batch orchestrates large-scale compute. FSv2 lacks InfiniBand RDMA. ACI unsuited for HPC workloads requiring inter-node communication.

questions.promptHtml: A containerized application requires running 10,000 short-lived tasks (30 seconds each) triggered by queue messages. Tasks are stateless and do not require orchestration. Solution must minimize costs and start tasks within 5 seconds of queue message arrival.

options.label: Azure Container Instances with Container Groups triggered by Azure Functions
Azure Kubernetes Service with horizontal pod autoscaling
Azure Container Apps with KEDA queue scaling

questions.correctLabel: Azure Container Apps with KEDA queue scaling
questions.extraHtml: Container Apps with KEDA automatically scales containers based on queue depth (0 to N instances). Subsecond cold start. Pay-per-execution pricing for consumption tier. ACI requires manual triggering and slower cold start. AKS over-engineered for event-driven tasks (requires infrastructure management).

questions.promptHtml: A legacy .NET Framework application requires Windows Server 2016, specific registry configurations, and COM components. Application must migrate to Azure with minimal refactoring. Solution must support auto-scaling and backup.

options.label: Azure Virtual Machines with custom VM images and scale sets
Azure App Service with Windows containers
Azure Container Instances with Windows containers

questions.correctLabel: Azure Virtual Machines with custom VM images and scale sets
questions.extraHtml: VMs support full Windows Server with registry, COM components, and legacy dependencies. Custom images capture configurations. VM scale sets provide auto-scaling. Azure Backup supports VM backup. App Service Windows containers don't support COM components. ACI unsuited for stateful legacy applications.

questions.promptHtml: A microservices application on AKS requires Blue/Green deployments with instant traffic shifting between versions, A/B testing with traffic splitting (90% stable, 10% canary), and automatic rollback on errors. Solution must integrate with existing Kubernetes infrastructure.

options.label: Azure Service Mesh (Istio) with virtual services and traffic management
Azure Traffic Manager with weighted routing
Kubernetes Deployments with rolling updates

questions.correctLabel: Azure Service Mesh (Istio) with virtual services and traffic management
questions.extraHtml: Service mesh (Istio) provides advanced traffic management: traffic splitting percentages, canary deployments, automatic retries/timeouts, and observability. Integrates natively with AKS. Traffic Manager operates at DNS level (slow propagation). K8s rolling updates lack fine-grained traffic control and automatic rollback.

questions.promptHtml: A distributed application requires asynchronous communication between 20 microservices with guaranteed delivery and ordering per message session. Messages contain order processing data requiring FIFO processing per customer. Peak load reaches 50,000 messages/second.

options.label: Azure Service Bus Premium tier with sessions and partitioning
Azure Event Hubs with consumer groups and partition keys
Azure Storage Queues with at-least-once delivery

questions.correctLabel: Azure Service Bus Premium tier with sessions and partitioning
questions.extraHtml: Service Bus sessions guarantee FIFO ordering per session (customer). Premium tier supports high throughput (50K+ msg/sec) with partitioning. Exactly-once delivery. Event Hubs for event streaming, not message queuing with FIFO guarantees. Storage Queues lack ordering and session support.

questions.promptHtml: A real-time notification system must push events from Azure services (Blob Storage, Event Grid, custom applications) to thousands of subscribers with automatic retry, dead-lettering, and filtering per subscriber based on event attributes. Events must be delivered within 1 second.

options.label: Azure Event Grid with event subscriptions and filters
Azure Service Bus with topic subscriptions
Azure Event Hubs with consumer groups

questions.correctLabel: Azure Event Grid with event subscriptions and filters
questions.extraHtml: Event Grid purpose-built for reactive event distribution with subsecond latency. Native integration with 20+ Azure services. Advanced filtering per subscription. Built-in retry and dead-letter. Service Bus for reliable messaging, not reactive event distribution. Event Hubs for high-throughput ingestion, not fan-out distribution.

questions.promptHtml: A web API serves 1 million requests per day with response data rarely changing. 80% of requests query the same 100 products. API response time must be <50ms. Backend database query time averages 200ms. Solution must minimize database load.

options.label: Azure Cache for Redis with cache-aside pattern and sliding expiration
Azure API Management with response caching policy
Application-level in-memory caching

questions.correctLabel: Azure Cache for Redis with cache-aside pattern and sliding expiration
questions.extraHtml: Redis provides distributed cache accessible across multiple API instances (unlike in-memory cache). Cache-aside pattern queries cache first, reads DB on miss, populates cache. Sliding expiration keeps hot data cached. APIM response caching limited to gateway level (doesn't reduce backend calls from multiple gateways). In-memory cache not shared across instances.

questions.promptHtml: A microservices application stores configuration (feature flags, connection strings, service endpoints) that changes weekly. 30 services must refresh configuration every 5 minutes without redeployment. Configuration must support versioning and rollback. Secrets must integrate with Key Vault.

options.label: Azure App Configuration with feature flags and Key Vault references
Azure Key Vault with secret versioning
Azure Storage with JSON configuration files

questions.correctLabel: Azure App Configuration with feature flags and Key Vault references
questions.extraHtml: App Configuration provides centralized configuration with dynamic refresh (no redeployment). Feature flag management with targeting rules. Key Vault references for secrets (not stored in App Config). Configuration versioning and point-in-time snapshot. Key Vault for secrets only, not app configuration. Storage files require manual refresh logic.

questions.promptHtml: A CI/CD pipeline deploys containerized applications to 10 AKS clusters across regions. Deployments must be GitOps-based with automatic reconciliation of cluster state with Git repository. Configuration drift must be detected and corrected automatically. Solution must support progressive delivery strategies.

options.label: Azure DevOps with Argo CD and Flux
Azure Pipelines with kubectl commands
GitHub Actions with Helm

questions.correctLabel: Azure DevOps with Argo CD and Flux
questions.extraHtml: GitOps tools (Argo CD, Flux) continuously monitor Git repository and reconcile cluster state (automatic drift correction). Support progressive delivery (canary, blue/green). Azure Pipelines with kubectl is imperative (no automatic reconciliation). GitHub Actions with Helm lacks continuous reconciliation and drift detection.

questions.promptHtml: A global web application requires intelligent routing of users to the nearest healthy Azure region based on latency, with automatic failover during regional outages. Solution must support priority-based routing with primary region preferred unless unhealthy.

options.label: Azure Traffic Manager with performance routing and endpoint monitoring
Azure Front Door with priority-based routing
Azure Load Balancer with cross-region support

questions.correctLabel: Azure Front Door with priority-based routing
questions.extraHtml: Front Door provides priority-based routing (prefer primary region) with automatic failover based on health probes. SSL offload, WAF, and CDN capabilities included. Traffic Manager uses DNS (slower failover detection). Standard Load Balancer regional only (cross-region LB limited scenarios).

questions.promptHtml: An Azure environment with 200 VMs across 10 virtual networks in hub-spoke topology requires centralized egress filtering with threat intelligence and deep packet inspection. All internet-bound traffic must be inspected with logging. Solution must support automatic scaling during traffic spikes.

options.label: Azure Firewall Premium with threat intelligence and forced tunneling in hub VNet
Network Security Groups with service tags
Network Virtual Appliance with UDRs

questions.correctLabel: Azure Firewall Premium with threat intelligence and forced tunneling in hub VNet
questions.extraHtml: Azure Firewall in hub VNet with UDRs in spokes forces all egress through firewall (hub-spoke topology). Premium tier provides threat intelligence, TLS inspection, and IDPS. Auto-scales to handle traffic spikes. NSGs stateless filtering, no deep packet inspection. NVA requires manual scaling and management.

questions.promptHtml: A hybrid application requires private connectivity between on-premises datacenter and 50 Azure VNets across 3 regions with transitive routing. On-premises should route to any VNet through single connection. Solution must support 5 Gbps aggregate throughput and BGP route exchange.

options.label: ExpressRoute with Virtual WAN hub and spoke
Site-to-site VPN with Virtual Network Gateway in each VNet
ExpressRoute with VNet peering mesh

questions.correctLabel: ExpressRoute with Virtual WAN hub and spoke
questions.extraHtml: Virtual WAN provides transitive routing across hubs and spokes (on-prem routes to any VNet through WAN hub). Single ExpressRoute connection to WAN hub reaches all spokes. BGP support for dynamic routing. Multiple VPN gateways don't support transitive routing. VNet peering mesh requires full mesh (N*(N-1)/2 peerings, not scalable).

questions.promptHtml: A latency-sensitive application requires load balancing across VMs in multiple availability zones with session affinity (same client to same backend). Health probes must check application-level health every 5 seconds. Solution must support both inbound and outbound NAT.

options.label: Azure Standard Load Balancer with zone-redundant frontend and health probes
Azure Application Gateway with cookie-based affinity
Azure Traffic Manager with geographic routing

questions.correctLabel: Azure Standard Load Balancer with zone-redundant frontend and health probes
questions.extraHtml: Standard Load Balancer supports zone redundancy, session affinity (source IP), configurable health probes (5-second interval), and outbound NAT. Low latency (Layer 4). Application Gateway operates at Layer 7 (higher latency). Traffic Manager DNS-based (unsuited for session affinity and zone-level load balancing).

questions.promptHtml: A web application on App Service requires private access to Azure SQL Database, Storage, and Key Vault without internet exposure. Application must resolve Azure service private IPs using DNS. VNet integration required.

options.label: App Service VNet integration with private endpoints and Azure Private DNS zones
App Service with service endpoints
Azure Application Gateway with backend pools

questions.correctLabel: App Service VNet integration with private endpoints and Azure Private DNS zones
questions.extraHtml: App Service VNet integration enables private connectivity. Private endpoints assign private IPs to SQL, Storage, Key Vault. Azure Private DNS zones provide name resolution to private IPs (e.g., mydb.database.windows.net resolves to 10.0.1.5). Service endpoints don't prevent public IP exposure. Application Gateway for inbound traffic, not outbound private connectivity.

questions.promptHtml: An Azure SQL Database migration from on-premises SQL Server 2017 (5 TB, 100 databases) requires minimal downtime (<5 minutes), schema compatibility validation, and automatic rollback capability. Migration must preserve logins, jobs, and alerts.

options.label: Azure Database Migration Service with online migration and Azure SQL Managed Instance
Azure Data Factory with incremental copy to Azure SQL Database
SQL Server backup/restore to Azure SQL Database

questions.correctLabel: Azure Database Migration Service with online migration and Azure SQL Managed Instance
questions.extraHtml: DMS online migration provides near-zero downtime with continuous replication. Managed Instance supports 100% SQL Server compatibility (jobs, logins, alerts). Migration assessment validates compatibility. Cutover supports rollback. Azure SQL Database doesn't support SQL Agent jobs. Backup/restore significant downtime (5 TB).

questions.promptHtml: A data center decommissioning requires migrating 500 VMs (Windows, Linux, various applications) to Azure within 3 months. Migration must assess dependencies, group workloads, and perform discovery. Solution must minimize refactoring and support wave-based migration.

options.label: Azure Migrate with dependency analysis and replication appliance
Azure Site Recovery with recovery plans
Azure Data Box with VM disk transfer

questions.correctLabel: Azure Migrate with dependency analysis and replication appliance
questions.extraHtml: Azure Migrate provides assessment (dependency mapping, grouping, sizing), agentless discovery, and Azure Site Recovery-based replication. Supports wave-based migration with testing. Aligns with Cloud Adoption Framework. ASR component of Migrate but lacks assessment. Data Box for data transfer, not VM migration.

questions.promptHtml: A web application with 10 TB SQL Database, 50 TB Blob Storage, and 20 VMs must migrate to Azure with <1 hour downtime. Network bandwidth to Azure is 1 Gbps. Solution must optimize transfer time and minimize costs.

options.label: Azure Data Box for initial data transfer with DMS/ASR for final sync
ExpressRoute with parallel data transfer
Azure Site Recovery for all workloads

questions.correctLabel: Azure Data Box for initial data transfer with DMS/ASR for final sync
questions.extraHtml: Data Box physically transfers 60 TB offline (faster than 1 Gbps network). Database Migration Service and Site Recovery perform final incremental sync during cutover (minimizes downtime to <1 hour). ExpressRoute requires time to provision and doesn't reduce total transfer time significantly. ASR alone slow for 60 TB initial replication over internet.
